label: Sermon on the Mount – Hearing and Doing
identity: Jesus speaks as the authoritative covenant Lord whose words define true wisdom and safety.
enactment: People either hear Jesus' words and do them or hear but do not act.
effect: Obedient hearers are like a house on rock that stands in the storm; disobedient hearers are like a house on sand that collapses under judgment.
description: Echoing Deuteronomy's 'life and death' choice, Jesus presents hearing and doing His words as the decisive hinge between stability and ruin.

label: Vine and Branches – Abide or Wither
identity: Jesus is the true vine and the Father is the vinedresser who desires real fruit from His people.
enactment: Branches either abide in Christ and His words or fail to abide and become fruitless.
effect: Abiding branches bear much fruit and glorify the Father; fruitless branches are taken away, wither, and are thrown into the fire.
description: This hinge intensifies the Deuteronomic pattern: life and fruit flow from abiding in Christ, while persistent fruitlessness leads to removal and judgment.

label: Sowing to the Flesh or Spirit
identity: God is not mocked; His moral order is stable and He rewards Spirit-empowered perseverance.
enactment: People either sow to the flesh (indulging sinful desires) or sow to the Spirit (doing good and walking by the Spirit).
effect: Sowing to the flesh reaps corruption; sowing to the Spirit reaps eternal life; perseverance in doing good will be rewarded in due season.
description: Paul restates the Deuteronomic hinge as a sowing–reaping pattern grounded in God's unmockable moral order.

label: Warning and Rest in Hebrews
identity: God is faithful and His promise of rest still stands, but unbelief shuts people out.
enactment: The community either exhorts one another daily and holds fast in faith or allows hearts to harden in unbelief.
effect: Believing enter God's rest; those who harden their hearts as in the wilderness fall short and face judgment.
description: Hebrews rereads the wilderness generation through a Deuteronomic lens, turning their failure into a present warning hinge for the church.
